entry.title: Ghost breaches: How AI-mediated narratives have become a new threat vector
entry.content:
Three incidents. No actual breaches. Full-scale crisis response. AI hallucinations are creating a new threat vector that most organizations have yet to prepare for. The post Ghost breaches: How AI-mediated narratives have become a new threat vector appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hims Breach Exposes the Most Sensitive Kinds of PHI
entry.content:
Threat actors breached the telehealth brand, and now they may know patients' personal health details. What could they do with that information?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
entry.content:
An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CSA: CISOs Should Prepare for Post-Mythos Exploit Storm
entry.content:
In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an "AI vulnerability storm" triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
entry.content:
Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax-filing deadlines.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft Bets $10B to Boost Japan's AI, Cybersecurity
entry.content:
The deal aims to accelerate AI adoption, train workers, and develop cybersecurity partnerships — the latest move by a hyperscaler to compete for sovereign AI and data centers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
entry.content:
Two recently fixed prompt injections in Salesforce Agentforce and Microsoft Copilot would have enabled an external attacker to leak sensitive data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Navigating the Unique Security Risks of Asia's Digital Supply Chain
entry.content:
Regulatory differences, interconnected digital ecosystems, and the rise of AI have created a complex supply chain Asian organizations must wrangle.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
entry.content:
While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
entry.content:
Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft Paid Out $2.3 Million at Zero Day Quest 2026 Hacking Contest
entry.content:
Researchers found more than 80 high-impact cloud and AI vulnerabilities during the event, which had a $5 million prize pool. The post Microsoft Paid Out $2.3 Million at Zero Day Quest 2026 Hacking Contest appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Splunk Enterprise Update Patches Code Execution Vulnerability
entry.content:
The flaw allows low-privileged users to upload files to a temporary directory to achieve remote code execution. The post Splunk Enterprise Update Patches Code Execution Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Data Breach at Tennessee Hospital Affects 337,000
entry.content:
Cookeville Regional Medical Center was targeted last year by the Rhysida ransomware group, which stole 500GB of data. The post Data Breach at Tennessee Hospital Affects 337,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OpenAI Widens Access to Cybersecurity Model After Anthropic’s Mythos Reveal
entry.content:
GPT‑5.4‑Cyber is a model fine-tuned for defenders, lowering boundaries for legitimate cybersecurity work. The post OpenAI Widens Access to Cybersecurity Model After Anthropic’s Mythos Reveal appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Government Can’t Win the Cyber War Without the Private Sector
entry.content:
Securing national resilience now depends on faster, deeper partnerships with the private sector. The post Government Can’t Win the Cyber War Without the Private Sector appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices
entry.content:
An indirect prompt injection could be chained with a sandbox bypass and Cursor’s remote tunnel feature for shell access to machines. The post Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ukrainian emergency services and hospitals hit by espionage campaign using new AgingFly malware
entry.content:
Hackers have targeted Ukrainian hospitals and local government bodies in a new espionage campaign using a malware tool dubbed AgingFly, researchers say.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cargo thieving hackers running sophisticated remote access campaigns, researchers find
entry.content:
Losses from cargo theft in North America rose to $6.6 billion in 2025, driven largely by digital attacks, according to the fleet management company Geotab.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
entry.content:
An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
entry.content:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: New AgingFly malware used in attacks on Ukraine govt, hospitals
entry.content:
A new malware family named 'AgingFly' has been identified in attacks against local governments and hospitals that steal authentication data from Chromium-based browsers and WhatsApp messenger. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Data breach at edtech giant McGraw Hill affects 13.5 million accounts
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters extortion group has leaked data from 13.5 million McGraw Hill user accounts, stolen after breaching the company's Salesforce environment earlier this month. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New ATHR vishing platform uses AI voice agents for automated attacks
entry.content:
A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hackers exploit Marimo flaw to deploy NKAbuse malware from Hugging Face
entry.content:
Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in Marimo reactive Python notebook to deploy a new variant of NKAbuse malware hosted on Hugging Face Spaces. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Recently leaked Windows zero-days now exploited in attacks
entry.content:
Threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed Windows security vulnerabilities in attacks aimed at gaining SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Man gets 30 months for selling thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts
entry.content:
23-year-old Kamerin Stokes of Memphis, Tennessee, was sentenced to 30 months in prison for selling access to tens of thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: MN: Spring Lake Park Schools Closed After Suspected Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Spring Lake Park Today reports: Spring Lake Park Schools in Minnesota were forced to close on Monday due to a suspected ransomware attack that disrupted the district’s computer systems. Local authorities are investigating the cybersecurity incident, which caused the abrupt shutdown of all schools in the district as a precautionary measure. … According to officials,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: GTA-maker Rockstar Games hacked again but downplays impact
entry.content:
Joe Tidy reports: Grand Theft Auto developer Rockstar Games has been targeted for a second time in three years by hackers. The data breach affecting the gaming giant was reported by cybersecurity news outlets on Saturday, after a group of hackers claimed responsibility for the hack. In posts viewed by outlets, the criminals said they... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Booking.com warns customers their private travel details may have been accessed by ‘unauthorised party’
entry.content:
Here we go again? Lara Pearce reports: Popular travel website Booking.com has warned customers that their personal information including booking details and names may have been accessed by an “unauthorised third party”. Booking.com is one of the largest digital travel companies globally, with more than 28 million accommodation listings worldwide. The company sent emails to some of its Australian... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: A Silent Threat, Loud Consequences: Ransom Group Hits Law Firms Hard
entry.content:
When the FBI issued a Private Industry Notice in May 2025 about the Silent Ransom Group (SRG) targeting law firms, they were not exaggerating. The image on the left side of this post is not a new geometric wallpaper. The green boxes represent law firm listings on SRG’s leak site. There are about 38 by... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: City of Anthony, NM, public records have been suspiciously disappeared, locked, or wiped
entry.content:
There are insider breaches, and then there are fourth-degree felonies and other possible charges if public records are destroyed improperly or without a lawful purpose. KVIA in New Mexico reports: The City of Anthony released a letter to KVIA on Saturday stating that the previous administration had allegedly committed several wrong-doings and the City is... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: California’s cybersecurity audit rule is now in effect: its impact for class litigation
entry.content:
The IAPP writes: Last year, the California Privacy Protection Agency adopted a major new rule requiring certain businesses to conduct an annual cybersecurity audit. The rule went into effect 1 Jan. 2026. This pioneering requirement, the first of its kind among state data privacy laws of general applicability, may entail substantial compliance efforts for affected companies to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: P3 Advertised 20+ Years and 0 Security Breaches. You Can Guess What Happened Next.
entry.content:
Introduction P3 Global Intel advertises itself as a “fully integrated and state-of-the-art tip acquisition and tip management solution that has quickly become the leading choice of Crime Stoppers Programs, Law Enforcement Agencies, Campus Safety Programs, and Federal Agency Initiatives.” 35,000 U.S. schools use P3 Campus, which partners with “safer school” initiatives such as Sandy Hook... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Defendant Sentenced To Prison For Hacking Betting Website
entry.content:
Not everyone complies with the conditions of pre-sentencing release. After pleading guilty to a credential stuffing attack, this defendant reopened his online criminal shop and advertised what he was doing. Unsurprisingly, he was re-arrested and remanded into federal custody. Although the DOJ announcement does not name the betting site, it was reported in news as... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Unpatched Microsoft Defender flaw lets hackers gain admin access
entry.content:
Laura Pippig reports: A security vulnerability was recently discovered in Microsoft Defender, the first-party Windows 11 antivirus tool used by millions. Attackers can exploit this vulnerability to gain elevated system privileges and cause significant damage without users noticing. The so-called “RedSun” vulnerability was discovered by security researcher Chaotic Eclipse, the same one who previously published a... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Double trouble: Hackers used both Claude Code and ChatGPT in a cybersecurity hack that lasted two and a half months.
entry.content:
Kenna Hughes-Castleberry reports: Nine Mexican government agencies were hacked in an artificial intelligence (AI)-driven cyber campaign between December 2025 and mid-February 2026 in what researchers have said should “serve as a wake-up call.” According to researchers at cybersecurity company Gambit Security, a small group of individuals used Claude Code and OpenAI’s GPT-4.1 to breach both... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Kettering Health Ransomware Attack: 1.7 Million Individuals Affected
entry.content:
In May 2025, the Ohio health system Kettering Adventist Healthcare (Kettering Health) experienced a ransomware attack. The attack was detected […] The post Kettering Health Ransomware Attack: 1.7 Million Individuals Affected appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free Webinar: How to Stop Phishing Attacks Before They Reach Your Team
entry.content:
Phishing has long been a leading cause of healthcare data breaches. Hackers target employees as they are a weak link […] The post Free Webinar: How to Stop Phishing Attacks Before They Reach Your Team appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Data Breach at Rocky Mountain Associated Physicians Affects 50,000 Patients
entry.content:
Rocky Mountain Associated Physicians has reported a data breach affecting more than 50,000 patients. Data breaches have also been announced […] The post Data Breach at Rocky Mountain Associated Physicians Affects 50,000 Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Stryker Cyberattack Has Impacted First Quarter Earnings
entry.content:
The Medical technology giant Stryker has provided an update on the impact of its March 11, 2026, cyberattack, confirming that […] The post Stryker Cyberattack Has Impacted First Quarter Earnings appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Brockton Hospital Ransomware Attack: Downtime Procedures to Continue for Two Weeks
entry.content:
Brockton Hospital in Massachusetts is continuing to grapple with a cybersecurity incident that took many of its electronic systems offline […] The post Brockton Hospital Ransomware Attack: Downtime Procedures to Continue for Two Weeks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Ransomware Attack on Cookeville Regional Medical Center Affected 338K Individuals
entry.content:
Cookeville Regional Medical Center in Cookeville, Tennessee, has recently confirmed that a 2025 ransomware attack exposed the personal and protected […] The post Ransomware Attack on Cookeville Regional Medical Center Affected 338K Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Anne Arundel Dermatology Pays $2.4M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Anne Arundel Dermatology has agreed to pay $2,400,000 to settle a consolidated class action lawsuit stemming from a cybersecurity incident […] The post Anne Arundel Dermatology Pays $2.4M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
